title: Add ground check before scaffold fallback goto in place action
description:
**Source:** External audit | **Council:** P2

**Problem:** At index.js ~1100, when all 4 preferred step-aside offsets have no walkable ground, the code falls back to:
```js
await bot.pathfinder.goto(new pfGoals.GoalNear(x + 2, y, z, 1));
```
This blindly walks to `x+2` with NO check for whether solid ground exists at that position. If that location is a cliff edge, hole, or lava, the bot could walk off and take fall/lava damage.

**Fix:** Before the fallback goto, check if the ground at `(x+2, y-1, z)` is solid (non-air, non-liquid). If not, try `(x-2, y, z)` as another fallback. If neither has ground, log a warning and skip placement with a `blockPlaceSkipped` event.

**Files:** `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` (~lines 1094-1102, in the place action)

**Validation:** Place a block at a position surrounded by air (no ground in any cardinal direction) — bot should log warning and skip, not walk off a cliff.
